mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates compress the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present values and boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between FEDFUNDS level and subsequent 60-day returns is not negative (Rank IC > 0.03) over any rolling 2-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6%, it signals the economy has entered a recessionary phase where labor market weakness reduces consumer income and spending, pushing corporate earnings down and equity prices lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE crosses above 6.0 the share of positive 20-day forward returns exceeds 50%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of a federal funds rate below 1% and a month-over-month decline in housing starts indicates that monetary easing has failed to revive the housing sector, a bellwether for durable demand, implying further equity downside over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If in periods with FEDFUNDS < 1 and falling HOUST the 20-day forward return is positive more than 30% of the time, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During NBER-defined recessions, declining industrial production signals a sustained contraction in aggregate output that depresses corporate profits and equity valuations over a 60-day horizon, amplified by falling capacity utilization.
failure_condition: If during recession the rank correlation between INDPRO monthly change and forward 60-day returns is not negative with absolute Rank IC below 0.03, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales growth (RSAFS) is a coincident indicator of consumer spending; a positive monthly change always foreshadows stronger corporate revenues and a rising S&P 500 over the next 20 trading days because consumer demand drives roughly two-thirds of economic activity.
failure_condition: If the sign of monthly RSAFS change matches the sign of forward 20-day returns less than 60% of the time over any rolling 3-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When both payroll employment and industrial production are contracting in the same month, it confirms a synchronized downturn in labor and output that historically precedes deep bear markets; such joint declines predict negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: monthly PAYEMS change < 0 and monthly INDPRO change < 0
failure_condition: If when both PAYEMS and INDPRO decline together the forward 60-day return is positive more than 20% of the time, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A housing starts level below 800 thousand units indicates extreme weakness in residential investment, which has outsized multiplier effects on employment and lending; this threshold crossing signals negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days because the housing bust remains unresolved.
failure_condition: If after HOUST drops below 800, the proportion of positive 20-day forward returns exceeds 40%, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary easing cycle, the acceleration of federal funds rate cuts (i.e., larger negative changes month over month) amplifies the positive impact on equity prices over a 5-day horizon as markets front-run further easing, providing a short-term positive momentum signal.
failure_condition: If during identified easing cycles the rank correlation between monthly changes in FEDFUNDS (negative) and forward 5-day returns is below 0.03 (absolute), reject.
